content.content:
Web search results for query: "webkit.org CSS masonry syntax blog post two options specification 2024"

Links: [{"title":"Help us choose the final syntax for Masonry in CSS | WebKit","url":"https://webkit.org/blog/16026/css-masonry-syntax/"},{"title":"Help us invent CSS Grid Level 3, aka “Masonry” layout | WebKit","url":"https://webkit.org/blog/15269/help-us-invent-masonry-layouts-for-css-grid-level-3/"},{"title":"Brick by brick: Help us build CSS Masonry | Blog | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/blog/masonry-update"},{"title":"Should masonry be part of CSS grid?","url":"https://ishadeed.com/article/css-grid-masonry/"},{"title":"CSS { In Real Life } | CSS Masonry Layout Syntax","url":"https://css-irl.info/css-masonry-layout-syntax/"},{"title":"An alternative proposal for CSS masonry | Blog | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/blog/masonry"},{"title":"CSS WG Blog – Masonry Spec Update and Open Issues","url":"https://www.w3.org/blog/CSS/2025/09/18/masonry-update-issues/"},{"title":"Masonry Feedback, Round 2 – Frontend Masters Blog","url":"https://frontendmasters.com/blog/masonry-feedback-round-2/"},{"title":"Feedback needed: How should we define CSS masonry? | Blog | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/blog/masonry-syntax"},{"title":"Masonry Layouts in CSS - The Cascade","url":"https://csscade.com/masonry-layouts-in-css/"}]

Based on the search results, I found the WebKit blog post you're looking for. Here's what I found:

The CSS Masonry specification is drafted with two options, going head-to-head — the "Grid Integrated" option and the "Grid Independent" option.

**Key details about the two options:**

1. **Grid Integrated Option**: This option integrates masonry directly into CSS Grid. Apple believes "Masonry" should be part of CSS Grid, as this functionality expands CSS Grid to create columnar grids and should be mixed with all the other features of Grid, including options for defining columns, track spanning, explicit placement, and subgrid.

2. **Grid Independent Option**: The Chrome team believes that a separate masonry syntax would be the best way to proceed. Rachel Andrew argued in favor of the Grid Independent option, noting that good defaults make things easier to teach and mean less configuration, and developers won't have to write as much code if this feature becomes its own display type with new default values suited for Masonry layouts.

The WebKit blog post about this was published in April 2024, with demos available at webkit.org/demos/grid3.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
